prompt: search the web for recent news about local llms
answer:
The search results show some articles and discussions about local LLMs. The dates seem a bit odd (some are in 2026, which is in the future relative to the current date of 2025-12-19, but this might be due to the search engine's indexing or the way dates are presented). Let me summarize the findings for the user.
</think>

Here are some recent articles and discussions about local LLMs:

1. **"Local LLMs are actually good now, and I wasted months not realizing it"** (XDA Developers) - An article discussing how local LLMs have improved significantly and are now viable for various tasks.
   [Read more](https://www.xda-developers.com/local-llms-are-good-now-wasted-months-not-realizing-it/)

2. **"This Local LLM Looked Smart Until I Saw What It Made Up"** (YouTube) - A video exploring the capabilities and potential hallucinations of local LLMs.
   [Watch here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zBYfzecY5ww)

3. **"Everything I've learned so far about running local LLMs"** (nullprogram blog) - A detailed blog post sharing insights and lessons learned from running local LLMs.
   [Read more](https://nullprogram.com/blog/2024/11/10/)

4. **Reddit r/LocalLLM** - A community discussion about local LLMs, including tips and experiences.
   [Visit subreddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/LocalLLM/)

5. **Facebook Group Discussion** - A thread discussing the top local LLMs for coding, highlighting models like GLM-4.5-air that run well on consumer hardware.
   [Read discussion](https://www.facebook.com/groups/1577315533418837/posts/1618059769344413/)

The general consensus is that local LLMs have become much more capable and accessible, running efficiently even on consumer-grade hardware.
